Transcrição Completa: A Foolproof Way To A Packed Pipeline In Just 60 Days

  

TRANSCRIÇÃO DETALHADA

 

[00:00] "All right, inner circle family. Let's do a quick mic check. That way I know that you can hear me loud and clear. So, please let me know in the Discord chat if you can hear me."

 

[00:15] "Welcome inner circle family to module two of the ultra high ticket closers. Are you ready?"

 

[00:30] "Tonight we have Rich Jay and Sean Lawrence who will be your instructors of today."

[01:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Module 2: A Foolproof Way To A Packed Pipeline In Just 60 Days"

NÚMEROS: "60 Days" (prazo do método)

VISUAL: Título principal em destaque, formato de apresentação profissional, design limpo focando no prazo de 60 dias como elemento central

[02:30] "For some of you, if you don't know me or you haven't seen a video pass by from me in the community, then my name is Donna Brasser. I am your community manager. I'm also your sister from season 8 and I am from Belgium."

 

[03:15] "My name is Rich. I am from season one long long time ago in a galaxy very far away. And I could say the same thing pretty much. HTC changed my life."

 

[03:45] "I'm the B2B sales manager for the Danlock organization sales teams. I'm a member from season 12 and kind of hit the ground running. Season 12 literally changed my life."

 

 

[05:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Background & Experience"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados numericamente neste slide

 

VISUAL: Apresentação dos instrutores com backgrounds profissionais - Rich (Dell, business school, closers.com, copywriters.com) e Sean (serial entrepreneur, technology startups, Virt Networks, Dragon 100)

 

 

[06:30] "My background actually, I did work for a corporate sales division at Dell which is kind of a larger company mostly on the sales operations side but I did a lot of process improvement."

 

[07:15] "I founded a publicly traded company called Virt Networks. We were a voiceover IP telecommunications company and the largest deal that I put together for that company was a seven-figure deal that spanned three countries and over 500 locations."

 

 

[08:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Ground Rules"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Lista de regras de engajamento: No distractions, Engage in Discord, Be resourceful, Show up each week (bi-weekly para UHTC)

 

 

[09:30] "Did you guys get around to watching and doing the homework, watching the video from Josh Kim? What would be one key takeaway for you guys from watching that video?"

 

[10:00] Respostas do chat: "Mindset, confidence, business acumen, strong confidence, don't fear the future, take action, just do it."

 

 

[12:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Have We Been Brainwashed?"

 

NÚMEROS: "54% more leads through inbound vs outbound, 62% less cost per lead" (estatística da HubSpot)

 

VISUAL: Citação destacada sobre marketing inbound vs outbound, com estatísticas de eficiência de custo e geração de leads

 

 

[13:15] "According to HubSpot, a leading inbound marketing and sales software, 54% more leads are created through inbound marketing versus outbound methods for a cost of 62% less per lead."

 

[14:00] "Have we been brainwashed? If you actually buy into that the concept of waiting around for leads, what does that mean for you guys as salespeople?"

 

[15:30] "Outbound calls and calling people's almost become a dying art. And it's because of some of these things that people have said out there that everybody believes that we can just rely on inbound marketing and what you'll find is that you can't just rely on it alone."

 

 

[17:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "What We're NOT Doing Today"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Lista negativa enfatizando o que NÃO fazer - não esperar por leads, não depender apenas de inbound, não ter mentalidade passiva

 

 

[18:30] "We're not going to sit there and wait for the leads to come. This is something I can't emphasize it enough guys because we come out of the program and we are literally roleplaying this over and over again but we're not really role playing proportionately right."

 

[19:45] "The inbound leads mentality is actually the reason why we teach this is because it's actually the easiest portion of being a salesperson, a closer, right? The most difficult part of all of this is actually flipping the psychology and saying, 'I actually have to work for this. I got to go out there and hunt.'"

 

 

[21:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Reality Check"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado numericamente

 

VISUAL: Imagem conceitual mostrando que o que você coloca no pipeline determina o que sai - representação visual de input/output de prospecting

 

 

[22:15] "The reality is it's all about work. What you put into the pipeline, how much you put into that pipeline actually determines what you get out of it."

 

 

[23:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Today's Objectives - 4 Key Areas"

 

NÚMEROS: "4" áreas principais

 

VISUAL: Lista numerada:

Two reasons to be an ultra high ticket prospector

Two mindset qualities necessary

Strategy: Intentional Frequency

Tactical applications

 

 

[24:30] "We're going to walk away from this session with four things. Just four, not too many. We're going to start with a basic foundation. We're going to talk about two reasons why you want to be an ultra high ticket prospector."

 

 

[25:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Quick Poll: First Thoughts on Prospecting"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Slide interativo solicitando primeira palavra/emoção sobre prospecting

 

 

[26:00] Respostas do chat sobre prospecting: "Money, power, hunting, outbound, closing, control, fanatical, growth, hard work, connecting, helping, serving, gatekeepers, outreach."

 

[26:45] "What would your lower self say about sales prospecting?"

 

[27:00] Respostas do lower self: "Intimidating, scary, difficult, waste of time, annoying, nervous, below me, prospect will hang up, monotonous, time draining, uncomfortable."

 

 

[28:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Deal Flow"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Conceito visual de fluxo de negócios, representação positiva vs negativa de prospecting

 

 

[28:30] Respostas sobre Deal Flow: "Money, hope, revenue, abundance, positive expectancy."

 

 

[29:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "B2C vs B2B Prospecting - Main Differences"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Comparação visual entre vendas para consumidor final vs vendas empresariais

 

 

[30:00] Respostas sobre diferenças B2C vs B2B: "Not personal, more technical, leverage, more sophisticated, business acumen, help versus mission, relevance, large deals, time, solution versus needs, deal size, pain points."

 

 

[32:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Gardening Metaphor"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Duas imagens de árvores lado a lado - uma murcha/fraca à esquerda, uma forte/florescente à direita, representando habilidades mal desenvolvidas vs bem desenvolvidas

 

 

[33:15] "Close your eyes and imagine if you have a garden and the different areas of your garden represent the different skill sets you have with you. What makes a tree grow like that versus a tree that's a bit more flourishing?"

 

[34:00] Respostas sobre o que faz árvores crescerem: "Nutrients, environment, roots, water, attention, sunlight."

 

[35:30] "B2B prospecting is going to be like one of your trees. We want your tree to look like the one on the right. It's not just environment. We actually want to change a little bit of the DNA of the seed that we're going to plant for this guy."

 

 

[37:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Prospecting Money Tree"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Diagrama completo de uma árvore com componentes rotulados:

ROOTS (Raízes): Motivadores

TRUNK (Tronco): Mindset inabalável

BRANCHES (Galhos): Estratégias

LEAVES (Folhas): Táticas

 

 

[38:15] "What we're trying to do here is start from the roots. We're going to talk about the things that motivate you to get up and become an ultra high ticket prospector. Then on top of that, an unshakable trunk. Then your branches are going to be your strategies. And finally, the smaller branches and the leaves are going to be your tactics."

 

 

[40:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Gem Types & Prospecting"

 

NÚMEROS: "4" tipos de gems

 

VISUAL: Referência aos 4 tipos de personalidade (Emerald, Pearl, Ruby, Sapphire) e sua relevância para prospecting

 

 

[41:30] "B2B prospecting, does it matter what gem type you are? Contrary to popular belief, if you go into a sales job and you do a DISC assessment or a gem assessment, a lot of sales directors and sales managers are actually hiring for a specific personality - Ruby."

 

[42:45] "I'm going to break the stereotype that to be a good prospector today you have to be Ruby and Sapphire. I don't think that's true. Our B2B team is made up of all different gem types."

 

 

[44:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "ROOT #1: Control The Data, Control The World"

 

NÚMEROS: "$300 billion" (valor do Google), "150-300 billion" (valor estimado do Facebook)

 

VISUAL: Logos de Facebook e Google no fundo, representando o poder dos dados, conceito de conhecimento como poder

 

 

[45:30] "Knowledge is power, right? Applied knowledge is power. How much are you guys paying attention to the value of some of these larger companies like Facebook, like Google?"

 

[46:15] "Facebook is worth somewhere above 150 billion, probably closer to 200 or 300 billion. What is it about Facebook that makes it so valuable? Social data. Attention. Client base."

 

[47:00] "Google's worth $300 billion itself. Not talking about Alphabet, the parent company, just Google. It's just a freaking search engine. How much could it possibly be worth?"

 

 

[48:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Rolodex"

 

NÚMEROS: "100,000 contacts" (tamanho do rolodex de Rockefeller), "$3.3 billion" (valor líquido de David Rockefeller), "5 feet high" (altura física do rolodex)

 

VISUAL: Imagens comparativas - rolodex físico antigo vs CRM moderno (HubSpot), representação visual de gerenciamento de contatos

 

 

[49:45] "David Rockefeller, American banker, chairman and CEO of Chase Manhattan Corporation. His fortune was estimated at 3.3 billion at the time of his death. His rolodex was five feet high. Can you imagine 100,000 contacts in your CRM that you can leverage?"

 

 

[52:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Calendar Example: Outbound Activity Schedule"

 

NÚMEROS: "20-30 dials" por sessão, "7:30 to 8:30", "9:30 to 11:30", "1:30 daily meetings"

 

VISUAL: Calendário detalhado mostrando blocos de tempo para discagem, com números específicos de chamadas por período, reuniões internas marcadas

 

 

[53:15] "This is an example of a salesperson's calendar. Somebody who's doing phone prospecting, phone outreach, mostly outbound. You can see the actual number of dials here on the calendar - 20-30 dials from 7:30 to 8:30, 20 dials from 9:30 to 11:30."

 

 

[55:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Numbers Game"

 

NÚMEROS:

"3 fruitful conversations/day = 60 data points/month"

"10 conversations/day = 200 data points/month"

"2% conversion rate"

"33x more likely to close"

"4 deals" resultantes

 

VISUAL: Tabela comparativa mostrando matemática de prospecting, cálculo de conversão, poder do compounding de conexões

 

 

[56:30] "If you had three fruitful conversations a day, in one month, you'd have 60 data points. If you did 10, you'd have 200 data points. With a 2% conversion rate, you're actually 33 times more likely to close a deal. That's around four deals."

 

[57:45] "Imagine you had 10k deal, a 25k deal. How many of us here could use maybe four or five more deals closed from a month's work? That's why we do what we do here."

 

 

[59:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "ROOT #2: Nobody Knows You - They Don't Owe You Anything"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Mensagem enfática em negrito, design impactante para reforçar o conceito de que prospects não devem nada ao vendedor

 

 

[1:00:15] "Really nobody out here knows you. You don't have your name established in the market. They don't owe you a goddamn thing. They don't owe you a phone call. They don't owe you a response to an email."

 

[1:01:30] "Every single business owner, every single consumer even is bombarded with information overload. People are asking for meetings, asking to sign this, asking replies on email. With all of that information overload plus the fact they don't really know you at all, they're not going to call you back."

 

 

[1:03:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Statistics: Sales Follow-Up Behavior"

 

NÚMEROS:

"48%" nunca fazem follow-up

"25%" fazem 1 follow-up

"12%" fazem 2 follow-ups

"10%" fazem 3 follow-ups

"80% of sales" são feitas entre o 5º e 12º contato

 

VISUAL: Gráfico de funil mostrando queda dramática em persistência, destaque para estatística dos 80% no final

 

 

[1:04:45] "Half of salespeople out there are never going to follow up with a prospect. A fourth might reply back and then it stops. Maybe 12% will do three contacts. But 80% of the sales are made on the fifth to the 12th time you contact that person."

 

[1:05:30] "If you're doing any less than that, you're missing out on a huge probability equation there. 5 to 12 times. What kind of patience does that take?"

 

 

[1:07:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "TRUNK Quality #1: Curiosity - The Search for Wisdom"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Representação visual de curiosidade, imagens de perguntas, exploração, mente aberta

 

 

[1:08:15] "Curiosity is really a mind that's open to possibility. This is the first unshakable quality of your trunk of the tree. A mind that's full of wonder."

 

[1:09:30] "I think I kind of like dealing with tough prospects and prospects that don't answer my phone calls, don't answer my emails. Because my next action, I'm thinking in my mind, is how can I solve this puzzle? How can I change my tonality, change my wording in my email?"

 

 

[1:11:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Curiosity Quote - Jeff Hoffman"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Citação em destaque: "When reps take the role of a curious student rather than an informed expert, buyers are much more inclined to engage."

 

 

[1:12:45] "When reps take the role of a curious student rather than an informed expert, buyers are much more inclined to engage. You got to be curious enough to ask the questions. If you're naturally curious, doesn't it come out more naturally?"

 

 

[1:14:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Information Gap Theory of Curiosity"

 

NÚMEROS: "1990s" (desenvolvimento da teoria)

 

VISUAL: Conceito visual de gap de informação, representação do desejo de preencher lacunas de conhecimento

 

 

[1:15:45] "Behavioral economist George Loenstein developed the information gap theory of curiosity in 1990s. Curiosity is a bit like the compelling urge to scratch an itch. It makes us want to seek out more information."

 

 

[1:17:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Curiosity in Action - Questions to Ask"

 

NÚMEROS: "1 more" (filosofia do "mais um")

 

VISUAL: Lista de perguntas reflexivas começando com "What if..." e "I wonder..."

 

 

[1:18:15] "What if I just called one more prospect? What if I replied to just one more email before I finish? What if I just took my dream 100 list and added 10 more?"

 

[1:19:30] "Sometimes we have to wonder. The wondering, the curiosity really drives that extra behavior. One more call. It's cumulative. It adds up."

 

 

[1:21:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "TRUNK Quality #2: Constant Improvement & Systematic Approach"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Representação de sistemas, processos repetíveis, melhoria contínua, ciclos de feedback

 

 

[1:22:30] "The mindset you got to have in addition to curiosity is how do you keep improving and you have to tie that to scaling. Sifue often asks us, 'I like what you're doing. What happens if we multiply that activity by 500? Does that break the system?'"

 

 

[1:24:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Customer Experience Matters"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado numericamente

 

VISUAL: Conceitos de experiência do cliente, jornada do comprador, pontos de frustração

 

 

[1:25:15] "I piloted one of the first elite sales support teams at Dell. We served the top corporate customers. One of the things we learned is experience matters. A confusing experience can cause a customer or business client to leave, to stop communicating."

 

 

[1:27:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Quality Quote - William Foster"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Citação destacada sobre qualidade não ser acidente

 

 

[1:28:15] "Quality is never an accident. It is always the result of high intention, sincere effort, intelligent direction, and skillful execution. It represents the wise choice of many alternatives."

 

 

[1:29:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Process Mapping Example"

 

NÚMEROS: Múltiplos timestamps e métricas de processo

 

VISUAL: Flowchart complexo com caixas, setas, decisões, tempos de resposta, mostrando processo de vendas detalhado

 

 

[1:31:00] "Each one of these steps represents a process. When you get to it, everybody's got a different process. You cannot have a set of customers you're contacting where it's taking five minutes for you to respond to their emails and then another set is taking two days."

 

[1:32:15] "What gets measured gets improved. If you can't measure it, if you can only describe that verbally and you can't put some measurements around that, you're not going to be able to improve it."

 

 

[1:34:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Dragon 100 Process Example"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados neste contexto

 

VISUAL: Referência ao processo sistemático usado pela equipe B2B de Dragon 100

 

 

[1:35:30] "How many remember we showed you guys a sneak peek at some of our processes for the B2B sales team? Why do we have that process? Consistency. Systematic. To increase value and quality. But what's the other thing we need to do with a process to know if it's effective? You have to be able to measure it."

 

 

[1:37:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Review: Two Roots & Two Mindsets"

 

NÚMEROS: "2" roots, "2" mindsets

 

VISUAL: Recapitulação visual dos fundamentos:

Root 1: Control the data

Root 2: Nobody owes you

Mindset 1: Curiosity

Mindset 2: Systematic improvement

 

 

[1:39:00] "How many of you remember the first motivator, the first root that creates your money tree? Control the data, control the world. And the second one? Nobody knows you. Nobody owes you anything."

 

 

[1:40:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "STRATEGY: Intentional Frequencies"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Conceito visual de frequência intencional, padrões repetitivos, cadência deliberada

 

 

[1:42:00] "Intentional frequencies - it's how often are you doing something? How often are you thinking about it? And it's measured. Prospecting is not happening to you. You have to make it happen."

 

 

[1:43:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sifue Quote on Commitment"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Citação: "There is no commitment without structure"

 

 

[1:44:45] "Sifue says there is no commitment without structure. If you're committed to becoming a high ticket prospector, you have to have structure."

 

 

[1:46:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Three Areas of Intentional Frequency"

 

NÚMEROS: "3" áreas principais

 

VISUAL: Lista:

How you start your days

Prioritizing

Calendar management

 

 

[1:47:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Morning Routine Quote - Sean Rhodes"

 

NÚMEROS: "8 a.m." como horário tarde demais

 

VISUAL: Citação sobre planejamento noturno: "If you wait for the morning, it's already too late. Best performers start planning before leaving work the day prior."

 

 

[1:49:15] "If you wait for the morning, it's already too late. The best performers actually start planning before leaving work the day prior. Pulling together your plan at 8 a.m. means your competitors already have a head start on you."

 

 

[1:51:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Morning Routine Quote - Andrew Thompson"

 

NÚMEROS: "10" to-do items, "3" success list items, "1" keystone item, "30 days" para resultados

 

VISUAL: Sistema de priorização em camadas: To-do list → Success List (top 3) → Keystone (most important)

 

 

[1:52:45] "Out of a list of 10 to-do items, you must select three prioritized items that must be accomplished that day - your success list. From your success list, you select one item that is the most important overall - your keystone. You begin your day with your keystone."

 

 

[1:54:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pearl Morning Routine Example"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Rotina matinal focada em bem-estar: alongamento, gratidão, água, café/chá, velas, sonhar acordado, intenções, emails

 

 

[1:56:00] "Schedule your day with intent. Before getting out of bed, I take a few minutes to stretch and express gratitude. I set an intention for the day. I drink a full glass of water, make coffee or tea, light candles, and daydream while setting more intentions."

 

 

[1:58:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Rich's Morning Routine"

 

NÚMEROS: "5" pés de altura (rolodex), "30 minutes" meditação (ideal), "15 minutes" (mínimo)

 

VISUAL: Lista sequencial detalhada:

Copo de água

Banho frio

Gratidão e meditação (30 min)

Lista de porquês pessoais

Metas do ano

Saldo bancário

Metas do trimestre

Calendário do dia

 

 

[2:00:15] "I start with a glass of water. Then I hop in a shower with cold water - the shock to my system. Then I do my gratitude and meditation for about 30 minutes. Then I look at my list of personal whys. After that, goals for the year. Then I force myself to look at my bank account balance every single day."

 

[2:02:30] "Even if I had the account balance for the day there, sometimes I still would not eat during that time. I was a big fan of the bulletproof diet - fasting in the morning until about 1-2 o'clock in the afternoon."

 

 

[2:04:00] MATTHEW THOMAS COMPARTILHA SUA HISTÓRIA

 

[2:05:30] "I remember just my whole life being raised and being told there was some virtue in us being poor. My mom was like, 'Yes, but we have these immigrant qualities.' But immediately conversely what that meant is that to have wealth was somehow something you should feel bad or guilty about."

 

[2:07:15] "Last night the IC class was for me one of the most meaningful educational experiences, maybe singularly the most for my entire life. Everything about self-esteem and being able to separate self-esteem from self-image and from self-confidence."

 

[2:09:00] "I look at my bank account and I felt anxiety. The anxiety has been killing me. I just got kudos for writing a letter that is an introductory letter because we didn't have that standardized sales material."

 

 

[2:12:00] ADAM NASSER COMPARTILHA SUA ROTINA DE 2 HORAS

 

[2:13:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Adam's 2-Hour Morning Ritual"

 

NÚMEROS: "2 hours" ritual total, "5:00 a.m." horário de despertar, "14 minutes 50 seconds" (Attitude of Gratitude), "21+" affirmations

 

VISUAL: Sequência detalhada:

Attitude of Gratitude (14:50)

Millionaire Mindset Affirmations

HTC Creed

Doder Affirmations

Personal Affirmations (21+)

Reading (Closer's Black Book)

Thinking Time

Whiteboard Planning

 

 

[2:15:00] "I get up latest at 5:00 a.m. The first thing I do is the attitude of gratitude which takes 14 minutes and 50 seconds. Then I do the millionaire mindset affirmations. Then the HTC creed and my personal affirmations."

 

[2:16:45] "I do this precisely because when you wake up, sleep is a trance state. After you wake up, you are in a trance afterglow. You are even more suggestible than you would be in trance. When I say my affirmations there, they have such a high impact."

 

[2:18:30] "Before I started those rituals, I felt like a lazy slacker. My day was completely out of my control. Now that I'm doing it, I feel that even though sometimes I have days where I'm not as productive as I want to be, I am constantly improving."

 

 

[2:21:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Prioritization: McKinsey Analytics Quote"

 

NÚMEROS: "53%" high performers effective with analytics, "37%" slow growers

 

VISUAL: Estatísticas comparativas sobre uso de analytics em organizações de vendas

 

 

[2:22:45] "Research by McKinsey confirms that analytics is playing an increasingly critical role in sales. 53% of sales organizations classified as high performing rate themselves as effective users of analytics compared to only 37% of organizations classified as slow growers."

 

 

[2:24:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lead Prioritization Factors"

 

NÚMEROS: "2 weeks old" vs "1 week old" (exemplo de idade de leads)

 

VISUAL: Conceitos de priorização: engagement level, trigger events, recency, lead scoring

 

 

[2:26:00] "How do you prioritize? When you have a lead that was two weeks old that you would call first before you would call one that was only one week old, there may be a trigger point where you say if this lead is actually reading the blog, engaging in the posts, filling out the application fully, you might want to reach out to that lead first."

 

 

[2:28:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Lead Decay Timeline"

 

NÚMEROS: "30 minutes, 1 hour, 2 hours" (janelas de engajamento decrescente)

 

VISUAL: Representação visual de como interesse de lead diminui com o tempo

 

 

[2:29:30] "If somebody just filled out an application form over the course of the next 30 minutes, one hour, two hours, what's happening to that person? Losing interest, distraction. We live in an information bombardment environment. 30 minutes later, my wife came by and showed me something else."

 

 

[2:31:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Non-Negotiables for Prioritization"

 

NÚMEROS: "5" princípios essenciais

 

VISUAL: Lista de regras não-negociáveis:

Always start with numbers

No such thing as cold outreach

Maintain dream 100 list

Monitor downstream effects

Create space for creativity/solution development

 

 

[2:33:15] "Always start looking at the numbers. Don't pick at random. You're doing it with intention. There's no such thing as cold outreach for us. Maintain your own dream 100 list because that is a reflection of a priority."

 

 

[2:35:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The 30-Day Rule (Predictable Revenue)"

 

NÚMEROS: "30 days" período crítico

 

VISUAL: Referência ao conceito de farming e efeitos downstream de parar prospecting

 

 

[2:36:45] "There's a great example in fanatical prospecting where one of the salespeople had a great calendar packed full of activities from November and then he actually stopped doing prospecting in December. By February, March, he was seeing the effects - his calendar was empty."

 

 

[2:38:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Calendar Management Fundamentals"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Conceitos de time blocking, batching, gerenciamento de energia ao longo do dia

 

 

[2:40:00] "Time blocking is simply the act of batching. When you do things in bulk, you tend to build up flow. You get the momentum. If you don't batch your activities, you're giving control back to the vacuum cleaner that's just going to suck your time."

 

 

[2:42:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Biggest Time Sucks"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Lista de desperdiçadores de tempo: emails, social media, smartphone notifications

 

 

[2:43:30] "What do you figure is one of the biggest most common time sucks out there? Emails, social media. The biggest time suck you carry in the palm of your hand - your smartphone. You hear a ding, you hear it vibrate, and what's the first thing you think? Oh god, I got to look and see what that is."

 

 

[2:45:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Non-Negotiables for Calendar Management"

 

NÚMEROS: "10" conversas/dia exemplo, horários específicos mencionados

 

VISUAL: Princípios de bloqueio de tempo:

Outbound calling in morning

Block research/creativity time

Batch high-intensity activities

Schedule email/messaging blocks

Respect time zones (Golden Hours)

 

 

[2:47:15] "Outbound calling in the morning. It sets the pace, it sets the tone. You typically have more energy when you start off in the morning so you can face some of those challenges of rejection better."

 

[2:49:00] "Emails and messaging - if you let it spin out of control, you'll feel like how come I didn't get anything done today? Block it in the calendar. How many times during the day are you blocking off activities related to emails and messaging?"

 

 

[2:51:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Response Time Expectations"

 

NÚMEROS: "1 hour" resposta esperada para mensagens

 

VISUAL: Conceito de expectativas de comunicação em diferentes canais

 

 

[2:52:30] "I tell my team that even though it's messaging, please don't expect me to respond any faster than an hour, one hour. If you wanted to get a hold of me in under an hour, what could you do? Pick up the phone."

 

 

[2:54:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Golden Hours Concept"

 

NÚMEROS: "12 hours difference" (exemplo Índia), "6-8 PM" (consumidores), "7 AM" (início para East Coast)

 

VISUAL: Conceito de horários ideais de contato por fuso horário

 

 

[2:55:45] "The concept of the golden hours - if you're calling in different time zones, maybe you've got to set that block of time where this is where I call. When I was calling overseas like India where it was 12 hours difference, my success rate went way up because I set aside that block of calls applicable to where I was calling."

 

 

[2:57:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Rich's Actual Calendar Template"

 

NÚMEROS:

"1.5 hours" outbound matinal

"11:00 a.m." food block

"1:30 PM" inbound calls start

"4-5 PM" documentation

"6-7 PM" research

"7-9 PM" additional outreach

"9-10 PM" planning next day

 

VISUAL: Calendário detalhado codificado por cores:

ROSA: Atividades outbound

AZUL: Outras atividades

Blocos específicos para cada tipo de atividade ao longo do dia

 

 

[2:59:15] "I left plenty of time for outbounding in the morning - about an hour and a half. I did emails and texts afterwards simply because I would know for a percentage of my calls I would have to send emails anyway."

 

[3:01:00] "I was a big fan of the bulletproof diet - fasting in the morning until about 1-2 o'clock in the afternoon. Even if I had the 11:00 a.m. food block there, sometimes I still would not eat during that time."

 

[3:02:45] "I do kind of slow down in the afternoon. That's why I love having my inbound calls start in the early afternoon - inbound calls are easier. Why not make sure that when you're sharp, you got full energy in the morning to go ahead and outbound?"

 

[3:04:30] "Documentation is key. I budgeted some time in the afternoon to follow up with my documentation - that's my 4 to 5 PM hour. Research time was 6 to 7 PM. A lot of the research for the next day's outbound calling happened between that 6 to 7:30 window."

 

 

[3:06:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Pipeline Math: 60-Day Example"

 

NÚMEROS:

"6 conversations/hour" média

"3.5 hours" outbound/dia

"1 out of every conversation" yields appointment

"60 appointments/month"

"10%" closing ratio

"6 deals in 60 days"

"1 deal every 10 days"

"20k-30k" deal size examples

 

VISUAL: Tabela de cálculos mostrando matemática completa do pipeline, projeções de conversão

 

 

[3:08:15] "If you assume on average six outbound conversations per hour, and we're doing 3.5 hours of outbound per day, and one out of every conversation yields an appointment, that's about 60 per month. If you're closing about 10%, that's six deals in 60 days."

 

[3:09:45] "This is not independent of marketing. This means I'm proactively reaching out to otherwise very dormant and inactive leads. These people probably have seen some kind of marketing materials - the better the marketing and branding, that's going to help."

 

 

[3:11:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Strategy Recap: Intentional Frequencies"

 

NÚMEROS: "3" componentes principais

 

VISUAL: Recapitulação da estratégia:

How you start your days

How you prioritize

How you manage calendar

 

 

[3:13:00] "This is your strategy - intentional frequencies. These are how you are operating. The frequency at which you are being intentional. Live your prospecting with intention."

 

 

[3:14:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "TACTICS: 5 Golden Rules for Written Outreach"

 

NÚMEROS: "5" regras essenciais

 

VISUAL: Lista de regras para email e messaging:

Don't send cold emails/texts

Focus on outcome, not process

Don't try to close via email

Keep it scannable and short

Time zone matters

 

 

[3:16:00] "Five golden rules for writing as an ultra high ticket prospector. Number one: don't send cold emails and texts. How do you know if it's cold? Not personalized, no research, no connection, no context."

 

[3:17:45] "Number two: We focus a lot of times on the process instead of the outcome. The last thing you say on the phone as an appointment setter is all that they're going to remember. Instead of saying 'Thank you, we look forward to talking to you on Wednesday,' structure it as 'Thank you, I look forward to talking with you Wednesday where we'll be going over how we can add 10 times the leads in half the time.'"

 

 

[3:20:00] SHNAIR COMPARTILHA EXPERIÊNCIA COM APPOINTMENT SETTING

 

[3:21:30] "That last half of the sentence is what they remember. Over the course of a hundred appointments booked, your show rate will be much higher than if you just said 'Thank you, bye-bye, I'll talk to you on Tuesday.'"

 

 

[3:23:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Rule #3: Don't Try to Close via Email"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Conceito de não fechar vendas high-ticket por email, manter foco em call to action apropriado

 

 

[3:24:45] "Are we saying you're not having a call to action in an email? No. Every email does have a call to action. However, is that call to action going to be closing a 20K deal? You're not going to close somebody on a 10K deal just by an email."

 

 

[3:26:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Rule #4: Keep it Scannable"

 

NÚMEROS: "Hundreds" de emails recebidos por dia (CEOs)

 

VISUAL: Conceito de capacidade de leitura rápida, subject lines, primeiras frases

 

 

[3:28:00] "B2B clients get tons of these in a day. Some cases hundreds of these a day. They're looking at your subject line and the first thing you got to say. If you can't catch instant gratification out of those two things, they've already moved on."

 

 

[3:29:30] SEAN COMPARTILHA EXPERIÊNCIA COMO CEO

 

[3:30:45] "As a CEO, half of the stuff I get sent, there's probably someone else in the organization that looks after that decision. I used to get hundreds of these a day. I had a favorite supplier and I memorized their phone number. As soon as I saw their number, the first thing I thought was 'Oh god, here we go.'"

 

[3:32:15] "They would call to tell me about their next webinar. Sometimes I was polite, other times I just hung up because I got frustrated. They would call me relentlessly every week, normally at least twice a week. Can you imagine what other CEOs are experiencing?"

 

 

[3:34:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Rule #5: Time Zone Matters"

 

NÚMEROS: "6:30 a.m." (exemplo de horário inadequado), "9-5" (horário de escritório)

 

VISUAL: Conceito de respeitar horários do prospect, ser prospect-centric

 

 

[3:35:45] "I would get people calling me at sometimes even 6:30 a.m. I haven't even really started my day yet and they're calling me looking to sell something. What do you think my first instinct was? I ignored it. If I did answer it, I had a few choice words for them and then I hung up."

 

 

[3:37:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "High Income Copywriter Course Recommendation"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Plug para curso de copywriting para melhorar habilidades de escrita

 

 

[3:39:00] "If you've never taken a writing course before, High Income Copywriter allows you to become more aware of how you write, understand where prospects are coming from, potentially close them into taking action. Don't venture out by yourself and send emails that are doing damage to your own brand."

 

 

[3:41:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Email Edge #1: Use Industry Verbiage"

 

NÚMEROS: "10" artigos recomendados para ler

 

VISUAL: Conceito de pesquisa de indústria, vocabulário específico, birds of a feather

 

 

[3:42:45] "Your outreach email has absolutely no industry verbiage whatsoever - that is not operating like an ultra high ticket closer. If you have not done your reading on the industry for your prospect, spend time, read at least 10 articles. Get to know your audience."

 

[3:44:15] "If you're writing to a medical community of doctors, they can tell whether or not you're part of their flock. When people are saying revenue, don't use the word sales. When people are saying sales, don't use the word income. You want to speak the same language."

 

 

[3:46:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Where to Find Industry Info"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Lista de fontes:

Website (não apenas homepage)

Press releases

LinkedIn articles

Industry news

Competitor analysis

 

 

[3:47:30] "Go to the website, but not just the landing page. Go into their press releases. Why? How often is their homepage getting updated? Probably not as often as their press releases. To demonstrate you know them, quote the press release."

 

 

[3:49:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Edge #2: The Referral Card"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Conceito de usar referências como hook mais poderoso

 

 

[3:50:45] "Why might it be interesting to explore the referral as an opener? Connection. It's personal. It is a hook. That's a direct connection of somebody you know, somebody you trust. It's a trusted source."

 

 

[3:52:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Bad Email Example #1"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Email fictício mal estruturado com subject line "You'll Love This!" e corpo genérico sobre novo programa

 

 

[3:54:00] "Hey, John. Hope you're well since we last spoke. We just recently launched a new program which I think you'll love. It's called the EdgeMax program for coaches..."

 

[3:55:15] "What's glaringly wrong with this? No value added at all. It's about me, me from the writer. There are typos. 'I hope you've been doing well since the last time we talked' - that is not a bridge."

 

 

[3:57:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "How to Bridge Properly"

 

NÚMEROS: "20-30 minutes" (exemplo de conversa anterior)

 

VISUAL: Exemplos de bridges efetivos que referenciam conversas/problemas específicos

 

 

[3:58:45] "If I had spoken to John before, I would say something like 'Last time we talked for 20-30 minutes about the fact that you wanted to explore this area, and I just noticed we launched this program so I wanted to see if this fits to solve that problem you have.'"

 

[4:00:15] "The difference is you made the actual event, the connection, the topic, the pain - you bring it back to light. Number one, I'm thinking as the recipient 'Hey, he remembers what we talked about.' Number two, 'He remembers I was trying to fix something.'"

 

 

[4:02:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "What If No Previous Data?"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Conceito de usar press releases e pesquisa para criar bridge

 

 

[4:03:30] "What happens if you don't have previous data? Research. If you see good news for a company in press releases - 'we just went public' or 'we just released a new drug' - what's a good way to open? Congratulating them."

 

 

[4:05:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Benjamin Sui Email Example (HIC Student)"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Email real de sucesso para Dragon 100, mostrando personalização e resposta positiva

 

 

[4:06:45] "Hey, Dominic, I reached out to you a while about Dragon 100. The reason was based on your cyber security leadership skills. The importance of cyber security is rising especially as the world seems to be moving more online."

 

[4:08:00] Resposta recebida: "Hi Benjamin, very humbled and appreciative of you reaching out. Happy to set up a time this week to chat further. I'm fairly flexible on Thursday and Friday. Be well and very much look forward to an epic combo."

 

[4:09:30] "Do you guys see the connection there? Having that understanding that shows you didn't just read the front page of their website - that's what got this response."

 

 

[4:11:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Templating vs Personalization"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Discussão sobre balancear templates com customização

 

 

[4:12:45] "If we're trying to be high output, balancing customization with being high performance, what does that mean about templates? The middle paragraph might be templated, but there are parts in the first and third paragraph you have to be sure you changed."

 

 

[4:14:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Email Success ≠ Response Rate"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Conceito de que "no" ou não-resposta não significa email ruim

 

 

[4:16:00] "Just because the business owner doesn't respond, does that mean your email is a bad email? No. If they reply 'no,' is that a bad email? No. It could be something as simple as it got caught in their junk folder or you're sending it right at the edge where their golden hour has passed."

 

 

[4:18:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Edge #3: Best Days/Times for Emails"

 

NÚMEROS: "Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday" (melhores dias), "Monday, Friday, weekends" (piores)

 

VISUAL: Estatísticas de abertura de email por dia da semana, baseado em 14 estudos diferentes

 

 

[4:19:45] "Most of the emails are still being opened not on Mondays, not so much on Fridays and weekends - it's still Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday. Why is Monday a difficult day? Busy, bad mood, start of the week, catchup. Monday tends to be a high meeting day."

 

 

[4:21:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Edge #4: Call to Action + Timeline"

 

NÚMEROS: "Friday" (exemplo de deadline)

 

VISUAL: Conceito de incluir valor, urgência e próximos passos claros

 

 

[4:23:00] "Call to action and timeline. Make it short and sweet. I'm delivering value to you. Here's when we can get this done for you by. For example, 'Until this Friday, we just opened this up for you.' What is the value I'm getting? How long am I getting it for? What's the call to action?"

 

 

[4:25:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sean's Real Email Example"

 

NÚMEROS: "1 hour" (tempo de resposta)

 

VISUAL: Email para lead de Danlock Ventures mostrando estrutura efetiva

 

 

[4:26:30] "Moses, I am one of Danlock's senior advisers responsible for handling requests sent to Danlock Ventures. I was forwarded your request looking for assistance to get your business ID off the ground. We do have something that would potentially be beneficial."

 

[4:28:00] "Please use the following booking link to schedule a call or reply by email with the best date and time to contact you. Please also confirm that is the best number to contact you at."

 

[4:29:15] "I sent this email out and I had a response within an hour. Notice I said 'reply by email with the best date and time' - what if that booking link doesn't work? That way they can still reach out to me."

 

 

[4:31:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Edge #5: The Hook"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Lista de tipos de hooks:

Referral

Industry event

Press release/acquisition

Competitor news

Award/promotion

Personal connection

 

 

[4:32:45] "Every email in one form or fashion, if you're going to get high open rates and high read-through rates, you got to hook with some level of interest. Is it an event for the industry? A report on acquisition? Maybe they merged companies recently. Is it a competitor?"

 

 

[4:34:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sean's LinkedIn Message Example"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Mensagem no LinkedIn para Roberta usando múltiplos hooks

 

 

[4:36:00] "Hey, Roberta, happy new year. Long time no see. I wanted to congratulate you on the successes you've had with your business. Looks like you've worked hard to get things off the ground and it has definitely paid off."

 

[4:37:30] "I'm reaching out because I need a favor. I'm working with a group in Vancouver to help launch a new initiative to help businesses scale in the face of disruption. As you've had some recent successes in the oil and gas sector, I wanted to get your input."

 

[4:39:00] "This is very powerful. There's a couple of layers. I would have thought Roberta was like Sean's long-lost friend. He's connected with a piece of news. He's very humble and vulnerable - 'I need a favor.' He's not pitching. He's asking for her opinion."

 

 

[4:41:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Bad Email Examples from Sean's Inbox"

 

NÚMEROS: "8 years experience" (Vikas)

 

VISUAL: Série de emails ruins recebidos como CEO

 

 

[4:42:30] Email #1 - Vikas: "My name is Vikas. I'm a professional website design developer with 8 years experience. Do you need a great website adjusted or built from scratch? Contact me if any of your work needs help."

 

[4:43:45] "The funniest part about this is if you actually went and looked at my website, I'm an IT consultant that helps companies build and scale their own websites. Is this really going to have a connection with me when I already have people that do this? No."

 

 

[4:45:00] Email #2 - Jenna: "Good day. This is Jenna from XYZ. I was wondering if you're available for a chat. I'm reaching out to see if your company would be interested in finding new clients."

 

[4:46:30] "Anybody got any ideas what's wrong with this email? Pitchfest. Not personalized. She emailed it to herself, which means she probably mass emailed this to everyone. 'I'm reaching out to see if your company would be interested in finding new clients.' No, I'm not interested. Why would I want that?"

 

 

[4:48:00] Email #3 - Jane: "This is Jane from Prima Housing China. We are one-stop service company for building materials. Mainly provide staircase, railing, glass, shower, kitchen, cabinet, bathroom, vanity, windows, doors, everything else."

 

[4:49:30] "I tuned out after 'This is Jane.' Actually, I tuned out after 'How are you?' Look at the end: 'May I have your WhatsApp or WeChat number for convenience? Mine is...' Kind of creepy. Needy is creepy."

 

[4:51:00] "My company has nothing to do with building materials. Spelling and grammar of emails is very important when you're talking to a business professional. Spellcheck and Grammarly are free - those tools will stop 98% of the mistakes you make."

 

 

[4:53:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "TEXT MESSAGING STATISTICS"

 

NÚMEROS:

"60%" aumento em adoção B2B

"60%" recomendam contato por texto no trabalho

"95%" textos de negócios lidos em 3 minutos

"90 seconds" tempo médio de resposta

 

VISUAL: Estatísticas destacadas sobre eficácia de text messaging para B2B

 

 

[4:55:00] "Despite low adoption rates, messaging for B2B is a trend we have to pay attention to. 60% of people recommend contacting them by text message at work. 95% of texts from businesses are read within 3 minutes of being sent. Average response time is 90 seconds."

 

 

[4:57:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Messaging Tips"

 

NÚMEROS: "2" dicas principais

 

VISUAL: Duas estratégias principais:

Leverage calendar invitation to confirm

Ask permission for free/fast communication

 

 

[4:58:45] "The first tip is leverage that calendar invitation if you booked a call to confirm it using messaging service. The second edge is this is a perfect opportunity to pave the road to having free and fast communication using this channel, but you got to ask for permission first."

 

 

[5:00:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Confirmation Text Example"

 

NÚMEROS: "Tuesday" (dia do appointment)

 

VISUAL: Série de mensagens mostrando confirmação e pedido de permissão

 

 

[5:02:00] "Hey John, I just got the confirmation for our meeting for next Tuesday to talk about how companies like yours are working with us to double the quality of leads in half the time. I've put the value proposition there because I'm going to say it over and over again."

 

[5:03:30] "Also, is this a good number to text you at? I just wanted to send you some mobile friendly material to jumpstart the conversation for Tuesday. If I wanted to push more material, I may confirm with them, ask permission, and then send something by text."

 

 

[5:05:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "WhatsApp Example - Sean"

 

NÚMEROS: "Saturday 2 PM" (horário agendado)

 

VISUAL: Conversa real no WhatsApp mostrando sucesso em alcançar prospect

 

 

[5:06:45] "Hello, it's Sean Lawrence from Danlock Education. I thought I'd try you on WhatsApp to see if this is a better way to get in touch with you. When do you have time for a 10 to 15 minute call?"

 

[5:07:30] Resposta: "Oh my god, I'm really sorry. I receive your phone call, but at the time I was working and I left my phone in the cabinet. May I have schedule a call on Saturday? Around 2 PM."

 

[5:08:15] "I couldn't for the life of me get a hold of this person by phone. I sent him this message and look at how quick I had a response. It goes back to: are you waiting around or giving up on this lead? No. He immediately opened up a second channel."

 

 

[5:10:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Re-engagement Text Example"

 

NÚMEROS: "Few months ago" (tempo desde signup)

 

VISUAL: Template para reengajar leads dormentes

 

 

[5:11:45] "Hey, John. It's Rich at XYZ company. Quick question. We saw that you signed up for our interest list for our lead gen services just a few months ago, and we just launched a few completely revamped services."

 

[5:13:00] "I'm not even sure if your company is still looking at a sales boost, but I thought I'd reach out. Are you still the right person to confirm this with? I'm using reverse psychology. I assume the worst. I'm not even sure if you're still the right person."

 

 

[5:15:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "LinkedIn Message Example - Sean"

 

NÚMEROS: "20 minute call" proposto

 

VISUAL: Mensagem LinkedIn com reverse outreach

 

 

[5:16:30] "Sorry we haven't got a chance to connect yet. I'd love to chat with you about Sky Corporation and how myself and my company can help get your website to the front page of Google."

 

[5:17:45] Sean's response: "Sure, we can have a call and chat. Actually, I'd like to get your input on a new project I'm working on as well. When would be a good time to catch up?"

 

[5:18:30] "Reverse outreach. The reverse close. I wanted his opinion on Dragon 100. People hate to be pitched, but people love to give their opinion."

 

 

[5:20:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Messaging Rule: Don't Be Too Available"

 

NÚMEROS: "5 minutes" vs "1-2 days" (extremos de tempo de resposta)

 

VISUAL: Conceito de manter batching mesmo com messaging

 

 

[5:21:45] "It's easy to get excited about going back and forth with a prospect. Finally, somebody responded. But as an ultra high ticket prospector, you are still concerned about your overall throughput. Go back to your plan schedule for contact."

 

[5:23:15] "Don't be the one who is hanging off of every single word of the prospect. If you got places where you're going to text anyway, just wait. Hold off. Somebody sent you an hour ago, wait until you get to the 9:00 window and then text them back."

 

 

[5:25:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Double-Check with Messaging"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Conceito de usar messaging para confirmar recebimento de emails importantes

 

 

[5:26:45] "Hey, I just sent you the quotation. Here's a couple of case studies as well. Would it be helpful if I reserve some time on the calendar to answer questions early next week?"

 

[5:28:00] "I'm starting off with confirmation of an event that happened using messaging. Sometimes you send it to email, it's lost. So you do email, confirm with a text. Or maybe it's text and then you confirm with the email."

 

 

[5:30:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Voice Messages on LinkedIn/WhatsApp"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Discussão sobre prós e contras de mensagens de voz

 

 

[5:31:45] "The problem with a voice message is it has to have some context to go with it. If somebody just blindly sends me a voice message with no context, I probably won't listen to it. Busy people don't have time to randomly listen to a voice message."

 

[5:33:15] "If I send a voice message to someone on LinkedIn, it's someone I normally already have some kind of connection with, or I've put a short message with it to give the voice message some context."

 

[5:34:45] "People can look over a voice message because it's not scannable. A text message, I can scan through it in milliseconds. If I want to hook my prospect, is it easier via voicemail or text message? You got to get them to click play first."

 

 

[5:36:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Messaging Timing Same as Email"

 

NÚMEROS: "Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday" (melhores dias)

 

VISUAL: Reforço de que princípios de timing aplicam-se igualmente a messaging

 

 

[5:38:00] "Your messaging based off of human behavior and business schedules usually follows the same principles for dates, for times as the email open rates. You really have to pay attention to your time zone."

 

 

[5:39:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Prospecting Money Tree - Complete Review"

 

NÚMEROS: "2" roots, "2" mindsets, "1" strategy, "multiple" tactics

 

VISUAL: Árvore completa mostrando todos os componentes aprendidos:

ROOTS: Control data, Nobody owes you

TRUNK: Curiosity, Systematic improvement

BRANCHES: Intentional frequencies

LEAVES: Email tactics, Messaging tactics

 

 

[5:41:15] "Back to what we talked about at the very onset. In this module we hit two reasons, two mindset qualities. How many of you guys remember? Control the data, nobody owes you. What are some ways you can be intentional? Intentional morning routine, time blocking, calendar."

 

 

[5:43:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Word Association Review"

 

NÚMEROS: Não aplicável

 

VISUAL: Revisão das palavras-chave discutidas no início: prospecting, deal flow, calling list, B2C vs B2B

 

 

[5:44:30] "How do you guys feel about those words now? Do you feel any different around sales prospecting, deal flow, contacting the list?"

 

[5:45:00] Respostas: "Better clarity, clear road map, perspective changes, it's just a process."

 

 

[5:46:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Module 2 Part 2 Preview - May 12th"

 

NÚMEROS: "May 12th" (data da próxima sessão)

 

VISUAL: Agenda para próxima sessão:

TACTICS: Video prospecting

Multi-channel tactics

Additional motivators

Advanced strategies

 

 

[5:48:00] "We're going to build on this in the next two weeks. An activity we do here on team Danlock is video prospecting - how do you actually use videos to get super high conversions? Multi-channel tactics. And we'll layer that on top of another set of motivators, mindset, and strategies."

 

 

[5:50:00] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "ASSIGNMENTS"

 

NÚMEROS: "6" assignments totais

 

VISUAL: Lista completa de tarefas:

Write personal list of whys

Rate yourself on curiosity (1-10) and systematic improvement

Post screenshot showing intention (calendar/routine)

Create list of companies (dream 100) with reasons

Create 5 emails

Create 5 messages

 

 

[5:52:00] "We got more assignments than you did from the intro class with Desmond. Number one: write down your personal list of whys. Number two: write down whether your curiosity as a salesperson and systematic improvements are there."

 

[5:53:30] "Number three: post one screenshot showing your intention as an ultra high ticket prospector. Could be your calendar, could be your morning routine."

 

[5:54:45] "Number four: create a list of companies - some of you have started on this. Describe why they made it to your list. Number five and six: create five emails and five messages. If you want feedback, post it to the Facebook community."

 

 

[5:56:30] "This is good exercise. I'm basically telling you guys to go to the gym. Do your push-ups, sit-ups, pull-ups, burpees. I hate burpees. I don't think many people like burpees."

 

[5:57:45] "This is part one of the foolproof way to a packed pipeline in just 60 days. We'll cover part two in two weeks. You are closer than you guys think. Talk to you guys soon. Have a good night."

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Pipeline Goal: 6 deals em 60 dias

Activity Metrics: 6 conversas/hora, 3.5 horas outbound/dia, 60 appointments/mês

Conversion Rate: 10% closing ratio = 1 deal a cada 10 dias

Deal Size: $10k-30k examples

Follow-up Stats: 80% de vendas acontecem entre 5º-12º contato

Email Stats: 95% lidos em 3 minutos, 60% preferem texto no trabalho

Data Value: Google $300B, Facebook $150-300B, Rockefeller rolodex 100,000 contatos

Calendar Example: 1.5h outbound matinal, 4-5 PM documentation, 6-7 PM research

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

 

THE PROSPECTING MONEY TREE:

ROOTS (Motivadores):

Control the Data, Control the World

Nobody Knows You - They Don't Owe You Anything

 

TRUNK (Mindset):

Curiosity - Search for Wisdom

Constant Improvement & Systematic Approach

 

BRANCHES (Strategy):

Intentional Frequencies (como você opera com cadência deliberada)

 

LEAVES (Tactics):

Email outreach (5 golden rules)

Text messaging (2 main tips)

Multi-channel approach

 

5 GOLDEN RULES FOR WRITTEN OUTREACH:

Don't send cold emails/texts (sempre crie ponte/conexão)

Focus on outcome, not process (último thing dito = remembered)

Don't try to close via email (CTA apropriado)

Keep it scannable and short (CEOs recebem centenas/dia)

Time zone matters (respeitar golden hours)

 

INTENTIONAL FREQUENCY COMPONENTS:

How You Start Your Days (morning routine)

Prioritizing (data-driven decisions)

Calendar Management (batching & time blocking)

 

PRIORITIZATION NON-NEGOTIABLES:

Always start with numbers/data

No such thing as cold outreach (sempre pesquisar)

Maintain dream 100 list

Monitor downstream effects (30-day rule)

Create space for creativity/solution development

 

ESTRUTURA:

 

MORNING ROUTINE FRAMEWORKS:

 

Rich's Routine:

Glass of water

Cold shower (shock system)

Gratitude & meditation (30 min ideal)

Personal whys list

Year goals review

Bank account balance (daily)

Quarterly goals

Daily calendar review

 

Adam's 2-Hour Ritual:

Attitude of Gratitude (14:50)

Millionaire Mindset Affirmations

HTC Creed

Doder Affirmations

Personal Affirmations (21+)

Reading (Closer's Black Book)

Thinking Time

Whiteboard Planning ($15K/month goal)

 

CALENDAR STRUCTURE:

Morning: Outbound calling (7:30-11:30)

Mid-day: Food break (11:00)

Afternoon: Inbound calls (1:30+)

Late afternoon: Documentation (4-5 PM)

Evening: Research (6-7 PM)

Night: Additional outreach (7-9 PM)

Planning: Next day prep (9-10 PM)

 

EMAIL EDGE STRATEGIES:

Use Industry Verbiage (read 10+ articles)

Leverage Referrals/Connections

Best Timing (Tue/Wed/Thu, avoid Mon/Fri)

Clear CTA + Timeline

The Hook (event/news/competitor/award/promotion)

 

MESSAGING TIPS:

Confirm calendar invitations

Ask permission for fast communication

Don't be too available (maintain batching)

Double-check important emails

Consider voice messages with context

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Target Deal Size: $10,000-30,000

Monthly Revenue Potential: $60,000-180,000 (6 deals × $10-30k)

Conversion Economics: 10% close rate on 60 appointments

Cost Comparison: Inbound 62% cheaper per lead vs outbound (HubSpot stat)

Data Asset Value: Rockefeller's $3.3B net worth largely from 100k contact rolodex

High Performer Analytics: 53% effective with analytics vs 37% slow growers

 

TEAM EXAMPLES:

Dragon 100 B2B process

Benjamin Sui (HIC student) - cyber security outreach success

Sean Lawrence - Virt Networks 7-figure deal, 500+ locations, 3 countries

Rich - Dell sales operations, closers.com, copywriters.com

 

 

DURAÇÃO: ~6 horas (incluindo breaks e role plays)

 

FORMATO: Treinamento ao vivo via Zoom com apresentação de slides, exemplos reais, participação da comunidade via Discord, role plays e compartilhamento de experiências pessoais

 

PRÓXIMA SESSÃO: Module 2 Part 2 - May 12th (Video Prospecting, Multi-channel Tactics, Advanced Strategies)
